seoTitle: Gilmore vs Gin Gin — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Gilmore and Gin Gin. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Gilmore vs Gin Gin: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Gilmore and Gin Gin.
heading: Gilmore vs Gin Gin — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Gilmore, New South Wales and Gin Gin, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Gin Gin has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Gilmore (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Gilmore or Gin Gin safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Gilmore has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Gin Gin scores 46/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Gilmore scores 63/100 for bushfire risk. Gin Gin scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Gilmore or Gin Gin?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Gin Gin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.95% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
